methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $3.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $8.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Astranis, a Series D space and aerospace company founded by John Gedmark and Ryan McLinko, carries a current post-money valuation of $3.5B following $350M+ in cumulative fundraising. The valuation assessment benchmarks against a comp universe of 8 private-stage peers in space and aerospace plus 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL), with notable private peers including Apex Space, Varda Space Industries, and Sierra Space.

Two valuation methods prove applicable to this stage. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $3.5B last post-money and applying a typical 1.5x Series D step-up multiplier, implies $5.2B. Peer-median analysis across 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields a median valuation of $1.1B, though the peer set displays substantial dispersion (range: $1.0B to $8.0B), reflecting heterogeneity in funding stage and market position within the space sector. Public-comparables anchoring via P/S multiple (median 42.9x across the 5 public comps) remains inapplicable due to unavailable revenue data.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods produces a range of $914M (low) to $6.0B (high), with a median implied value of $3.2B. The current $3.5B valuation sits above the median, supported primarily by stage-progression logic. Key uncertainties include the high dispersion in peer valuations—reflecting differentiated technology maturity and addressable markets—and the absence of revenue visibility to benchmark public-market multiples.
